|model=[[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]]<ref name="ISD1">{{ShadisCite|14|Strange Bedfellows}} describes the ''Magellan'' as an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] that had sixty [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, sixty [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and ten [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|tractor beam projectors]]. The [[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|Second Edition]] of the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' establishes that the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] had sixty turbolaser batteries, sixty ion [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]], and ten [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors—specifically [[Taim & Bak/Legends|Taim & Bak]] [[XX-9 heavy turbolaser/Legends|XX-9 heavy turbolasers]], [[Borstel Galactic Defense/Legends|Borstel Galactic Defense]] [[NK-7 ion cannon/Legends|NK-7 ion cannons]], and [[Phylon Transport/Legends|Phylon Transport]] [[Q7 tractor beam projector/Legends|Q7 tractor beam projectors]] per {{StarshipsVehiclesCite|5|In Command}}—whereas the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]], according to the ''Imperial Sourcebook'' and ''The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 5, had only twenty ion cannons but also at least one hundred turbolasers. Therefore, the ''Magellan'' must have been ''Imperial I''-class.</ref>

The '''''Magellan''''' was a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] that its [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Julias Narn]], used to ensure security at the Imperial [[Lunar Station]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Danton VI]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents blackmailed Narn into assisting them with retrieving hijacked supplies and captured Rebel personnel from [[Galderian/Legends|Galderian]] [[Galderian Slave Traders|enslavers]] operating in Danton VI's [[Trebodar system|star system]]. After he had invited the Rebels onboard the ''Magellan'' in order to formulate a strategy for their joint [[Mission to the Trebodar system|mission]], Narn used the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] to engage in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] the Galderians' [[Space ARC battle cruiser]] and lure it away from their [[Unidentified Trebodar system asteroid|asteroid base]] while the Rebels infiltrated the facility.

==Description==
The ''Magellan''<ref name="Shadis" /> was an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]]<ref name="ISD1" /> that was a part of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|navy]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Shadis" /> Manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and measuring 1,600 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length,<ref name="DB" /> it was armed with sixty [[Taim & Bak/Legends|Taim & Bak]] [[XX-9 heavy turbolaser/Legends|XX-9 heavy turbolasers]], [[Borstel Galactic Defense/Legends|Borstel Galactic Defense]] [[NK-7 ion cannon/Legends|NK-7 ion cannons]], and [[Phylon Transport/Legends|Phylon Transport]] [[Q7 tractor beam projector/Legends|Q7 tractor beam projectors]].<ref name="ISD1" /> The [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer's]] complement included a [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] of seventy-two [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/ln space superiority starfighters]].<ref name="Shadis" />

==History==
===Guarding a party===
Despite having little combat experience, the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] [[Julias Narn]] became [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Magellan'' due to his organizational abilities and his ability to command respect from his [[Soldier/Legends|troops]]. Narn kept his family at the Imperial [[Lunar Station]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Trebodar system]]'s [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Danton VI]], and the presence of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] in the vicinity of the relatively lightly guarded facility deterred any security incidents.<ref name="Shadis" />

{{RPG|"[[Strange Bedfellows]]"}}
At one point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Galactic Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Narn was present at a reception he had organized at the Lunar Station for visiting dignitaries. While there, he was approached by a team of Alliance agents who blackmailed him into assisting them with retrieving hijacked [[antibiotic]]s and supplies and liberating captured Rebel personnel from [[Galderian/Legends|Galderian]] [[Galderian Slave Traders|enslavers]] who were operating in the Trebodar [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The captain then invited the Rebels onboard the ''Magellan'' in order to plan their joint [[Mission to the Trebodar system|mission]].<ref name="Shadis" />

===Bait===
[[File:JuliasNarn-S14.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Captain Julias Narn (left) of the ''Magellan'' assisted a team of Rebel Alliance agents in dealing with a group of Galderian enslavers.]]
After Narn's [[Scout ship/Legends|scout ships]] had located the [[Slaver/Legends|enslavers']] base, which was located on an [[Unidentified Trebodar system asteroid|asteroid]] in the system's [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid cluster]], the captain formulated a plan: the ''Magellan'' would draw away the Galderians' 1,000-meter–long [[Space ARC battle cruiser]] and engage it in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]] while the Alliance team infiltrated the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] and retrieved the supplies and the captured Rebels. In the case where the supplies and the captives were instead situated aboard the [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battle cruiser]], Narn would do his best to damage the enslavers' [[Cruiser/Legends|starship]] without destroying it—although privately, wary of the prospect of potentially liberating numerous [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] crewmembers in the Trebodar system's [[Sector/Legends|sector]], the captain intended to either destroy the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] or chase it out of the sector anyway.<ref name="Shadis" />

When their plan was set in motion, the Rebel agents observed from a distance the ''Magellan'' approaching the asteroid cluster. In order to lure the Space ARC away from the base, it sent two [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighters]] toward it. The Galderians' [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] subsequently returned fire, and eventually it and several squadrons of Galderian [[I-BEAM Star Fighter]]s followed the Imperial warship away from the enslavers' asteroid, providing the Rebels with less than one [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] to infiltrate the asteroid, complete their objective, and escape.<ref name="Shadis" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Shadis14-Cover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Magellan'' was introduced in ''Shadis'' 14.]]
The ''Magellan'' appeared in "[[Strange Bedfellows]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure written by [[John Zinser]] and published in the [[July]]–[[August]] [[1994]] [[SHADIS Magazine 14|fourteenth issue]] of the [[SHADIS Magazine|''Shadis'']] magazine for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Since that adventure was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Shadis" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The adventure suggests that, depending on the outcome of the battle between the ''Magellan'' and the Space ARC battle cruiser, the Galderian warship can return to the enslavers' asteroid base, or, if the Star Destroyer prevails, Narn can demand that the player characters return to the ''Magellan'' for transportation back to Danton VI—a request that the player characters can ignore at the risk of earning the enmity of the captain. This article assumes that the adventure plays out as described.<ref name="Shadis" />
